session.user:
Perform a migration of all `RealLocalGitHub.for_test()` call sites across the codebase at /home/runner/work/erk/erk.
## What happened The `for_test()` classmethod was removed from `RealLocalGitHub` (in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/real.py`). We need to create a replacement standalone function and update all callers.
## Step 1: Create the replacement function
Add a `real_github_for_test` function at the END of the file `tests/test_utils/context_builders.py`. The function should be:
```python def real_github_for_test( *, time: Time | None = None, repo_info: RepoInfo | None = None, ) -> RealLocalGitHub: """Create RealLocalGitHub with test defaults.
Replacement for the removed RealLocalGitHub.for_test() classmethod.
Args: time: Time implementation (defaults to FakeTime) repo_info: Repository info (defaults to None)
Returns: RealLocalGitHub configured with FakeGitHubIssues """ from tests.fakes.gateway.github_issues import FakeGitHubIssues from tests.fakes.gateway.time import FakeTime
return RealLocalGitHub( time=time if time is not None else FakeTime(), repo_info=repo_info, issues=FakeGitHubIssues(), ) ```
You'll also need to add these imports to the top of `tests/test_utils/context_builders.py`: ```python from erk_shared.gateway.github.real import RealLocalGitHub from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import RepoInfo from erk_shared.gateway.time.abc import Time ```
Read the current file first to see exact placement.
## Step 2: Update all callers
There are ~50 call sites across these files: - tests/unit/gateways/github/test_real.py - tests/unit/gateways/github/test_parse_check_runs.py - tests/integration/test_real_github.py - tests/integration/test_real_github_pr_enrichment.py - tests/integration/test_github_graphql.py - tests/integration/test_helpers.py (just a docstring example) - tests/core/operations/test_github.py
For each file: 1. Replace `RealLocalGitHub.for_test(` with `real_github_for_test(` 2. Add import: `from tests.test_utils.context_builders import real_github_for_test` 3. If the file imported `RealLocalGitHub` solely for `for_test()`, and no longer uses `RealLocalGitHub` directly, remove the `RealLocalGitHub` import. But ONLY if `RealLocalGitHub` isn't used elsewhere in the file (type hints, other calls, etc.).
## Important details - The `for_test()` call takes keyword args `time=` and `repo_info=` — preserve these exactly - Most calls are `RealLocalGitHub.for_test()` with no args - Some calls pass `time=fake_time` or `repo_info=RepoInfo(...)` - The tests/integration/test_helpers.py reference is in a docstring — update it too - Do NOT change any non-import, non-call-site lines
session.assistant: I'll help you perform this migration. Let me start by reading the current state of the files and understanding the scope of changes needed.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Shared context builders for test scenarios. 2→ 3→This module provides reusable context builder functions to eliminate duplication 4→across test files. ... [123 lines omitted] ...

46-def test_parses_timestamps_from_iso_string() -> None: 47- """Valid ISO timestamp strings are parsed into timezone-aware datetimes.""" 48: github = RealLocalGitHub.for_test() 49- repo_info = RepoInfo(owner="owner", name="repo") 50- -- 61-def test_uses_epoch_sentinel_when_timestamps_missing() -> None: 62- """Missing timestamp strings fall back to the epoch sentinel datetime.""" 63: github = RealLocalGitHub.for_test() 64- repo_info = RepoInfo(owner="owner", name="repo") 65- -- 74-def test_extracts_author_from_user_login() -> None: 75- """Author is extracted from the user.login field.""" 76: github = RealLocalGitHub.for_test() 77- repo_info = RepoInfo(owner="owner", name="repo") 78- -- 85-def test_author_empty_when_user_is_none() -> None: 86- """Author is empty string when the user object is None (e.g., deleted account).""" 87: github = RealLocalGitHub.for_test() 88- repo_info = RepoInfo(owner="owner", name="repo") 89- -- 147-def test_parse_plan_prs_returns_both_draft_and_non_draft() -> None: 148- """Both draft and non-draft PRs are returned (no draft filtering).""" 149: github = RealLocalGitHub.for_test() 150- repo_id = GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo") 151- --

1→"""Tests for GitHub operations.""" 2→ 3→from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import ( 4→ _parse_github_pr_url, 5→ extract_owner_repo_from_github_url, 6→) 7→from erk_shared.gateway.github.real import RealLocalGitHub 8→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId 9→ 10→ 11→def test_parse_github_pr_url_valid_urls() -> None: 12→ """Test parsing of valid GitHub PR URLs.""" 13→ # Standard format 14→ result = _parse_github_pr_url("https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/23") 15→ assert result == ("dagster-io", "erk") 16→ 17→ # Different owner/repo names 18→ result = _parse_github_pr_url("https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/12345") 19→ assert result == ("facebook", "react") 20→ 21→ # Single character names 22→ result = _parse_github_pr_url("https://github.com/a/b/pull/1") 23→ assert result == ("a", "b") 24→ 25→ # Names with hyphens 26→ result = _parse_github_pr_url("https://github.com/my-org/my-repo/pull/456") 27→ assert result == ("my-org", "my-repo") 28→ 29→ # Names with underscores 30→ result = _parse_github_pr_url("https://github.com/my_org/my_repo/pull/789")

1→"""Tests for RealLocalGitHub with mocked subprocess execution. 2→ 3→These tests verify that RealLocalGitHub correctly calls gh CLI commands and handles 4→responses.
